reviews.review_description: this game is gorgeous for $10 at this is a steal but i really wish you could build with water and set trees also adding glass would be nice (maybe all of this is in the game I'm to dumb to find it) the world currently does feel a bit lifeless maybe adding a few basic fish in the ocean or a few land animals would be nice i'm not saying turn this into a survival game but it would be nice to have things running around as you build

developer_response.body: Hi there, thank you very much for your review! I'm building all of the above, the first feature requests will be in shortly and I'm also at work on animals. You can follow that progress on the game's youtube channel: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UC_IzsI78ai_9k4k7koA76BA

reviews.review_description:
Pocket lands is such a great game will potential! However there are a few problems that I found that could improve the game, such as:

It’s lonely: There’s not wildlife or animals and it feels empty.I already know animals are coming and I can’t wait!

The water is weird: You can’t swim in it, and when you go to place a block in the water and remove it, it creates a air pocket so maybe make the water a little more realistic. 

Build hight: it feels kinda low and maybe needs to get extended a bit higher.

Settings don’t save: When switching between player mode (Small guy) and architect mode (That’s what I’m calling the god mode.) Settings like the visibility reset back to what they first were. It would be nice if you would fix that!

Nature blocks: There are trees around the map that you can grab however, I can’t find them in the build menu. Adding trees or other nature blocks to the build menu would be great!

Anyways that’s all I have, hopefully you read this and it helps out a lot on what do it!

developer_response.body:
Hey midnightvr474, thanks for playing the early access of Pocket Lands! You're right, I've shifted priorities to adding NPCs first of all, as that is what most players are missing. You can follow the progress on the Pocket Lands YouTube channel. As for the water, propagating water will definitely come in a future update, but as a solo dev I can only work on one thing at a time! You can swim in it, however. Simply look at where you want to go and press forward.

Pocket Lands was designed as for the diorama mode first and foremost, which is why you always start in that mode. But I recognize that maybe some people will always prefer being immersed, and want to restart in immersed mode right away. I'll take that into account, thanks for the suggestion. As for your other suggestions: those are already planned!

reviews.review_description: I wasn't sure about this one but the positive reviews swayed me to pick it up. As of now I regret the purchase. There is very little content here. I'd like to see more interactive things to put into the world. Animals, NPCs, some furniture maybe? Honestly I find it boring, lonley, and depressing to play. I do see potential if more content is added and the dev seems nice so reluctantly I'm giving 3 stars even though I want to give 2. I like the idea but there needs to be living things added for me to have any desire to return to my pocket land. I'm just not impressed with this title, maybe I should lower my expectations.

developer_response.body: Hi there, thanks for your review. I'm sorry to hear you're not liking what is there so far, as the store page is very clear on what is included and what is coming up, as are the other reviews. The game is priced to what is currently available: a beautiful sandbox world you can play in a unique diorama mode in MR, or in full immersion in VR. Rest assured that animals, NPCs, furniture, multiplayer and more are in the works!

reviews.review_title: Its relaxing but still early access
reviews.review_description: I like the diorama mode very much! But as this game is still early access it gets boring and depressing very fast. More blocks, NPCs, animals and special blocks like a fence or fireplace would make this game much better! Thats why for now only 3 stars.

developer_response.body: Hi! Thanks for your review. The game is indeed sold as early access, at an early access price. As a solo developer, I'm doing the best I can to add more content as quickly as possible. Thanks for understanding and supporting further development!

reviews.review_title: Must play for relaxing/cozy games fans🌳
reviews.review_description:
This game is a must own if you like minecraft, it's very relaxing to be able to build little worlds in your living room and then be able to visit them in full scale and explore them.

The building mechanics are great, very easy to understand and to use, but I'd love to be able to have more blocks to build with. The music and ambient is very well done, totally immerses you. Overall the game is extremely recommended, the developer behind it is very kind, and I see a very bright future to the game! ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

developer_response.body: Thanks mausy! More blocks are coming soon 😎

reviews.review_description: This game is early access, I know it's limited but for what's in the game so far I will give it 5 stars in a heartbeat. It's very promising and the developer answers every question, if you like to build stuff from the ground up then you can give this one a chance before it gets expensive, if survivor and living creatures is your thing then it's not there as of yet

developer_response.body: Hi fenixdrs, thank you very much for your kind review!

reviews.review_title: Thomas Van Den Berge! What a developer!
reviews.review_description:
10/ 5 stars! ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
The fastest purchase I every made on Launch Day! You MUST add this to your library now! 

I just started playing and I stopped to make this review. Just wow!
Genuinely, I was emotional.
Try this: double tap B button to fly. Fly to the sun ☀️ Keep flying... what you are doing is not flying to the sun, but journeying to the dark side of the planet where the sun ☀️ actually sets behind the horizon, while the atmosphere color and tone changes as you approach the dark side of the planet! What? Wow deep development here.

This is more influential to VR amd MR as CUBISM was.

I won't explain the features as the Tutorial does an awesome job of guiding you.

What I'll say is I came across this app on X/ Twitter and I wishlisted Pocket Lands right away. 

This is an amazing experience...

I can't wait for the social element to be available to share, visit and meet others in new pocket lands.

Pure brilliance. 

Thanks TVDB ✌️

developer_response.body: Wow, thanks for the glowing review Malak!

reviews.review_description:
I always enjoyed creating stuff in Minecraft, but never liked the actual building process. Pocket Lands to the rescue! While you CAN build in the world directly, IMHO the game really shines in diorama mode, where you place blocks like Lego bricks, which makes the building process very enjoyable.

More block types will only make it better in the future and I'm very curious how the multiplayer building battles will work. Can't wait! :)

developer_response.body: Hi Mettanine, I'm happy to hear you that! More blocks are on the way!

reviews.review_description: Am I blind or is there not a way to delete worlds? Also, a way to name them would be nice. And some sort of way to spawn at your last building point. Ive lost so many projects because I get distracted and all turned around. But it's a great game.

developer_response.body:
Hi, thanks for your review! Great suggestions, rest assured they will all come in the upcoming patch this week. I'll let you know when it's up!

Update 30 Jan: All your requested features are in today's update! Let me know how you like them.
Update 7 Feb: Hi! Have you been able to check out the new features?
Update 30 Mar: Hi there! Did you take another look at the new features, including upside down steps?

reviews.review_description:
I’m hooked. I’m very glad that I got into it right away after it released, I immediately began having a ton of fun. My wife and kids are interested in it too, as they’ve watched me playing while casting to the TV. I’m glad to know that multiplayer and creatures roaming will be coming! Being that the game is in its early stages this certainly isn’t a complaint, but I would love to see world to world invites among players and HP/Lung Capacity along with some form of NPC combat sequences (wild animals, creatures, etc) added in the future. Keep it up, devs, this is an amazing game!

ETA: After reading developer replies to other reviews I’ve realized that this is a solo development project. For me that adds a huge WOW factor into the whole process. 10/10 Thomas!

developer_response.body: Hey Redd, Thomas here, thank you very much for your review! I'm hard at work to add more features. Thanks for your support!

reviews.review_title: Build a world on your coffee table
reviews.review_description: Pocket Lands is so much fun, especially for a Minecraft fan like me. I absolutely love being able to switch around the different viewpoints, and the mixed reality diorama mode is so cool. I like being able to use both controllers and hand tracking. The controls are pretty intuitive and the hand tracking gestures work pretty well. Not enough games utilize hand tracking, but this one is developed with it in mind. Everything runs smooth on Quest 3, and it looks great!

developer_response.body: Hi wolfpup64, thank you for your kind review! I'm happy to hear you're enjoying mixed reality diorama and the hand tracking, those were big design challenges.

reviews.review_description: It's a great little game with a lot of potential. I know it is an early development but there is not a lot of building materials like furniture. It would be good to have some type of enemy or monster to defend against.

developer_response.body: Hey there, thanks for your review. Don't forget that you're buying an early access release with well defined features as given on the store listing. Much more is on the way, but you get what you pay for currently, which is a beautiful voxel sandbox in diorama or in first person.

reviews.review_description: the game at its core seems interactive. The visuals are wonderful.  lacking content.  needs more basic blocks.   its hard to get creative.  feels like its repetitive. Alot of potential.  reminds me of no mans sky.   a ton of land but a little u can do with it.  i really hope it gets success.  thank you  i do recommend due to dev interaction and replies from buyers.  ill be patient nad cant wait to see what happens in the near future.  great job

developer_response.body: Hi Werbs, Thomas here, the developer of Pocket Lands. Thank you for your kind review! I'm back at the desk now after a very busy Holiday period in which I also moved house, so now it's full steam ahead with new content. New blocks, new shapes and new features coming right up!

reviews.review_description: I love this game. I just want more out of it. I hope the devs see how popular it is getting and decide to add to it with more updates. It would be fun to have other shapes of objects to increase the detail in your builds. Multiplayer would be nice. Maybe add farms and crops. Any way, good job with the game.

developer_response.body: Hi, thank you for your review! More is on the way, as a solo developer I'm giving it all I got. All your wishes are planned for development!

reviews.review_description: I mean what is there really to say? A TON. This game is absolutely fantastic there are so many different comfort settings that you can change. You can build your own worlds in your room and see around you, or go all in and experience the world with a gorgeous skybox. The controls are intuitive and unique and the possibilities are endless. The randomly generated worlds that included villages and castles really brings it all together. Can’t wait for future updates!

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for your glowing review 5StarLemon! That really makes my day. Do note that while the worlds are fully procedurally generated, there are no generated buildings yet. The castle and village are made by a player. Generated villages are planned for a future update though!

reviews.review_description: I really want doors and windows and some sort of farm animals to start with and then eventually villagers that would really make this game for me especially if they added some sort of aquatic Life for now I'm giving it five stars but hopefully within the next couple monthsthey can add some sort of life even if it's literally just chickens or just fish one moving thing would be pleasant also I would like fences and doors

developer_response.body: Thank you for your kind review! I'm on the case, I'll be adding all of the above and more in the coming months.

reviews.review_description:
great game but defo needs mpre content for the price, it has great potential as a VR version of minecraft just wants more blocks, animals, adding lava and crops to grow for a more interactive play. Even include mobs and weapons.

But definitely worth a play and hopefully the developers keep adding to it.

developer_response.body: Hey there, thanks for your review! More content is coming soon, starting with more blocks. I released it in early access to get feedback to steer the development direction, as well as give early adopters a low price of entry. At full release, the price will be $19.99 rather than $9.99.

reviews.review_title: It's a great idea, but it's a shame that
reviews.review_description: The game is cool, but its a shame the worlds arent based on Minecraft Java or Bedrock worlds. I think a great feature would be letting VR players design maps and then import those maps into Minecraft. Also, in general there arent many building materials but the potential is there.

developer_response.body: Hi, thanks for your review. Rest assured that more blocks are coming very soon! Regarding your feature request for basing Pocket Lands worlds on Minecraft worlds: I understand where you are coming from, but I hope you understand that they are different games by different studios, with different content. Pocket Lands is not a Minecraft world editor, it's a game of its own. I would appreciate it if you judge it that way!

reviews.review_description:
Minecraft has often been described as "digital legos".  Pocket Lands gives you both: you can build at your lego table in MR diorama mode and then you can walk through what you just built.  The magic of that never cease to make both this middle aged father and his children say "wow".

I love what's here already and can't wait to see how the game evolves.  Thank you dev!

Also, it's so cool to fly through the world using your head to steer.

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for your kind review! Hope you and your family have a lot of fun. Can't wait to add co-located multiplayer so you can actually build together!

reviews.review_description: I really love so many things about this game. Hopefully we can eventually get doors, furniture etc!

developer_response.body: Thank you very much! Doors, furniture, NPCs and much more are all on the way!

reviews.review_description: Playing in mixed reality is a fantastic experience! The feeling of building with Lego bricks on a table and the ease of rotating and moving the area around without needing to move is incredible. But once you drop the pin in the world and you go flying into it… WOW! I was smiling ear to ear and just so full of joy like a kid again. It gave me that magical feeling from the first time I ever played Minecraft and realized its potential. That’s what this game has already, a ton of potential and it’s only in Early Release, so that potential will only grow and grow. Don’t think twice about skipping this one, it’s easily one of the best VR experiences already and priced extremely fair!

developer_response.body: Hey there, thank you very much for your review! Stoked to hear you're having so much fun in the early access already.

reviews.review_description: Sorry I haven't been playing it But I will see if it's working. I just took a quick break,Thanks.

developer_response.body:
Hey there, the first block in the catalog is the lamp, which emits light. Smaller sources like torches are coming soon. Hope that helps!

Update 7 Feb: Have you been able to find the lamp block? Thanks!

reviews.review_title: MR/VR sandbox off to an Amazing Start!
reviews.review_description:
Just want to say this is almost flawless for an early release.

The only things I’d love to see added are an option to return to the main menu and multiplayer support (which I understand is already in development).

Beyond that, the game runs smooth as butter, and the mixed-reality diorama elevates the experience into something really special. The gameplay mechanics you’ve already established feel solid and thoughtfully designed, and I’m genuinely excited to see where this goes.

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for your review! I'm hard at work to keep building on the foundations laid for Pocket Lands.

reviews.review_description: Since I got the meta quest ive wanted a game just like this, what you have created is just beautiful. Guessing the sort of development route you will be taking (based on a somewhat similar game concept) this will end up being one of the biggest games in VR and will probably be the reason many people get into VR in the future. If I wasn't already sitting when I booted it up then I would have needed to as very few times in my 40 years of gaming has anything made me audibly gasp with wonder!

developer_response.body: Hi Ben, thank you very much for your kind review!

reviews.review_description:
This is an amazing experience of a sandbox game. I love the interactiveness and the likenesses to Minecraft but also with a whole different feel the mixed reality is a great experience and I love it. It personally looks awesome and it’s fun to make my own buildings and creations and share them with friends. I recommend this a lot and you should buy this no matter what you like. I’m sure you will enjoy this!
￼
I really like it keep it up

developer_response.body: Thanks Noah!

reviews.review_title: Fun to build worlds in my living room
reviews.review_description: I really like the atmosphere created and look forward to future additions to the world.

developer_response.body: I'm glad you like it so far!

reviews.review_description:
Honestly, I don't have any criticism. The game on early release was so smooth and good. Sadly, there aren't enough hours in my day to mindlessly roam the world. 
Love it! Hope it only gets better from here!

developer_response.body: Thanks Caramel, I'm hard at work to make it the best it can be!

reviews.review_description:
Hello! It's me from the reddit comment, Brilliant_Sun1103.

If reddit isn't where you mostly check, I'd love to put my suggestion here. Just as a future note if you wished for any suggestions! ^^

I love it, it looks so beautiful and feels so good to play in. I'd love the idea of taking inspiration from Vermillion (your past game), and placing elements from it into your newer game, in order to distinct it from the Minecraft clone title articles usually give.

If it's okay with you, I'd like to suggest world altering things, so that your pocket lands could all feel distinct from each other.

Like the ability to oil paint your own blocks and mobs (these can be templates, you never have to create a mob making system, I know it's hard.)

Or make a custom world system where you can change the hue of the backdrop (perhaps by slider or even paints if you can) or the style of the biomes.

I think this could lead to more creative variety without losing the artistic philosophy the game currently has. 

But for now, I love it!! Please keep up the good work, and dont let comments like mine divert you from your vision, I hope that it becomes something big! ^^

reviews.review_title: I really like this game but
reviews.review_description: Plzzz add multiplayer it would be so fun if I could build a mansion with my friends

developer_response.body: Hi ozzie12658, multiplayer is definitely going to be added before the full launch in 2026!

reviews.review_description:
Needs more options on the menu
More indoor decor 
And a survival mode

developer_response.body: Thanks Liam, more content is on the way! This is an early access release, priced at $9.99 so it's cheap for you to get the game early and be part of the development process, giving direction in what content you want most.

reviews.review_description:
Hey devs! Thanks for creating this game. The hand gestures work brilliantly and I find myself using my hands rather than controllers mostly. I am working on a cool build right now and it is addictive. 

I had some questions and the team answered them promptly which has bumped this up to a top experience. Thanks again :)

Look forward to what you all push out soon. The

Best wishes,

John

developer_response.body:
Hey John,

Thomas here, the solo dev of Pocket Lands. Thanks for your review, I'm happy to hear you're having such a great time!

1. Yes, by dropping the little blue shovel into the selection area. You'll find it next to the orange VR mode pin when a selection area is created. I should highlight that in the tutorial as well.
2. Yes, stair blocks will get corner steps and the top steps will get rounded corners!
3. That's coming soon, no worries!

Looking forward to earning that fifth star. ;)

Best,

Thomas

reviews.review_description: Amazing can we have more fun with it it's exactly what it says it is except it doesn't have doors but it's still amazing has so many new features plus I hope they add doors and furniture

developer_response.body: Hey mate, thanks for the review! Yes, doors and furniture will be added as the early access progresses!

reviews.review_description: A very relaxing game for building in MR and really cool to explore in full VR. Looking forward to future updates!

developer_response.body: Hey there, thanks for checking out Pocket Lands!

reviews.review_description: This incredible game is a must have for your VR library. Its fun and relaxing. One of the few i would give a 10/10.

developer_response.body: Thank you very much for your glowing review!
